item.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
item.description: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

item.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
item.description: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

item.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
item.description: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

item.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

item.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
item.description: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

item.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

item.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
item.description: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

item.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

item.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
item.description: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
item.description: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

item.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
item.description: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

item.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
item.description: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

item.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
item.description: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: The Agent Inside
item.description: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
item.description: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: No Return: Australia's Missing Billions
item.description: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

item.title: Report finds NT residents feel less safe and less satisfied with police despite crime crackdown
item.description: A new report reveals NT residents felt less safe over the past year, despite a government crackdown on crime.

item.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson was originally tapped by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in the weeks after the Bondi terror attack to lead a review into the federal government's intelligence and security agencies.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
item.description: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

item.title: Former royal commissioner casts doubt on antisemitism, Bondi Beach terror attack inquiry timeline
item.description: Ronald Sackville who led the disability royal commission says it is unlikely the the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion can properly examine the experiences of Jewish Australians before its December 2026 deadline.

item.title: NBL owner Larry Kestelman prepared to walk away from Wilkinsons Point land purchase
item.description: After the Tasmanian government was urged to abandon the sale of prime waterfront land but chose to persevere, it now appears the developer is prepared to "walk away" from the potential purchase.

item.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
item.description: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

item.title: Owners apply to develop Mackay's historic Eimeo Hotel into resort-style project
item.description: The Eimeo Hotel, known for its coastal views and community history, is at risk of being knocked down and locals are worried about the loss of a venue they view as a "Mackay institution".

item.title: The nannasphere is an antidote to the world's grief and horrors
item.description: Surely one of the best ways to counter the hate, grief and horrors of the world is to foster communities driven by decency, calm and care. Enter: the nannasphere.

item.title: The children feared to be at highest risk of missing out under Thriving Kids
item.description: With just months to go until Thriving Kids starts, parents and experts are warning a particular cohort of children is at risk of falling through the cracks.

item.title: She Matters memorial to women allegedly killed by men removed from Hosier Lane
item.description: The founder of Australian Femicide Watch says she is heartbroken over the removal of the She Matters mural in Hosier Lane dedicated to more than 200 women who have died since January 2025.

item.title: Feral cat numbers spark bounty push across outback Queensland
item.description: Regional councils are paying for feral cats as populations boom in outback Queensland, but experts are not convinced the strategy will help.

item.title: El Niño in 2026 could be one of the strongest on record — what does it mean for Australia's weather?
item.description: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly, thanks to the probable emergence of an El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires could be on the way.

item.title: Mixed reaction to Chinese-backed company's purchase of Kestrel Coal Mine
item.description: Yancoal Australia will acquire a majority stake in what it says is the "largest producing" underground coal mine in the country. The announcement has been met with a mixed reaction.

item.title: Anthony Albanese meets with Sultan of Brunei to shore up fertiliser and fuel supplies
item.description: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

item.title: Why invasive plants can harm your garden and what you can grow instead
item.description: Agapanthus, morning glory or gazanias might be easy-growing plants in your home but their invasive nature can damage your garden and the environment. Here's what experts recommend.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: Man faces court charged with culpable driving causing death after Melbourne pedestrian death
item.description: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

item.title: Love story: I fell in love with my daughter's father two years after she was born
item.description: After a casual fling led to a surprise pregnancy in my mid-20s, I was parenting alone. A phone call two years later changed everything.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 20: Can you tame our tricky questions?
item.description: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

item.title: Kakadu Aboriginal corporation to take over as operator of Jabiru Airport
item.description: Jabiru Airport had been slated for demolition in October this year, but after months of uncertainty, a new operator has emerged to take over.

item.title: Tow truck driver fined $5,000 over 2023 crash that killed man on Forrest Highway
item.description: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

item.title: Turtles 'awaiting their fate' buried under mud at NSW wetlands after inflows halted
item.description: Researchers in north-west NSW are wading through thick mud in an attempt to save hundreds of turtles after the NSW government decided to stop supplying water to the protected wetland.

item.title: Body found in New Zealand after deluge causes major flooding in Wellington
item.description: Police have found the body of a man as floodwaters recede in New Zealand's capital and the clean-up begins.

item.title: Resources companies fight gas tax push at parliamentary inquiry
item.description: An inquiry into the taxation of gas resources has heard from proponents for a tax, who believe Australians are being robbed of their fair share of wealth from the nation's natural resources, and opponents, who argue touching tax settings would spook investors.

item.title: Mitch Marsh 50 not enough as Lucknow Super Giants fall to fourth straight Indian Premier League defeat
item.description: Australia T20 skipper Mitch Marsh did his best to bail out Lucknow Super Giants, but his half-century wasn't enough to save them from defeat against Rajasthan.

item.title: Whale rescuers prepare for increased entanglements during annual migration
item.description: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

item.title: Dozen more Aboriginal burial sites detected amid construction on Rottnest Island
item.description: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

item.title: Failing on social mobility 'is inefficiency with a human face'
item.description: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

item.title: With Mojtaba Khamenei a 'symbolic sovereign', who is running Iran?
item.description: After a wave of assassinations targeting top Iranian officials and a supreme leader who has not been seen in public since the war began, a key question has emerged: who is really running Iran?

item.title: How the NDIS grew to four times the size that was expected
item.description: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

item.title: Donald Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as Middle East peace hopes fade
item.description: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

item.title: Dawson fights back tears after return as GWS edge Roos amid ARC drama
item.description: Adelaide skipper Jordan Dawson breaks down in tears after playing his first match since his brother's tragic death, as GWS prevails against North with the aid of a major ARC blunder.

item.title: Trump is political kryptonite in Australia but diplomacy still guides the way
item.description: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

item.title: Why street artist Peter Drew decided to bring back his 'Aussie' poster series
item.description: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: Australia's minimum fuel reserves would double under $800m Coalition plan, at a cost to drivers
item.description: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Penny Wong will have to navigate China, Japan and South Korea tensions
item.description: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
item.description: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
item.description: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

item.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
item.description: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

item.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
item.description: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
item.description: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

item.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
item.description: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

item.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
item.description: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.

item.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
item.description: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
item.description: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

item.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

item.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
item.description: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 1: Are you a vexillologist in the making?
item.description: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?
item.description: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

item.title: Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion interim report delivers 14 recommendations
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion releases its interim report, making 14 recommendations.

item.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
item.description: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

item.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

item.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
item.description: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
item.description: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

item.title: Chalmers does little to stop capital gains tax debate as budget looms
item.description: Seven years ago, proposed changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing were named as part of the reason Labor lost the unlosable election. Times have changed.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla says it faced IDF interception
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man accused of attending Mount Gambier businesses while nude faces court
item.description: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

item.title: NSW strikes late to win Game I as Origin series heads to Queensland
item.description: NSW has come from behind to win the opening match of the Women's State of Origin series, and their coach is looking forward to trying to win the shield in Queensland.

item.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz, but PM says 'no determination' made
item.description: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

item.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested
item.description: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a stunt gone wrong, a burger blue and a rescued roo
item.description: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

item.title: Bond yields at crisis level as Australian government's 10-Year Treasury rate passes 5 per cent
item.description: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, pushes past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

item.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
item.description: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

item.title: ACCC case against Woolworths winds up as spotlight remains on integrity of grocery pricing
item.description: As the Woolies federal court case wraps up and the Coles matter awaits judgment, here's what we've learned so far from the high-stakes cases.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: Commanders to brief Trump on fresh military options for Iran war — as it happened
item.description: Iran says it will respond with "long and painful strikes" on US positions if Washington renews attacks and reasserts its control over the Strait of Hormuz — as it happened.

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: National average house prices rose slightly in April, the slowest pace of growth since January 2025
item.description: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

item.title: China using a double-insurance strategy to secure crude oil supplies amid Iran war
item.description: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pacific nations aim to tackle regional unrest with expanded Pacific Response Group
item.description: The Pacific Response Group is beginning negotiations on a treaty that would allow it to respond more quickly to natural disasters and other crises across the region.

item.title: 'No secrets': Alternative school's history of discipline in spotlight
item.description: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

item.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says
item.description: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

item.title: Once-safe Coalition seat Farrer now in sights of One Nation at by-election
item.description: A fascinating four-cornered contest is brewing as voters in the southern NSW seat of Farrer head to the ballot box for the second time in 12 months.

item.title: Taylor says government is in 'denial' about situation in NT town camps — as it happened
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says the federal government is in "denial" about the situation in town camps following the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened
item.description: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

item.title: Emma Thompson stars in a new film made for an orchestral performance of Holst’s The Planets
item.description: A new silent film starring Emma Thompson and Greg Wise will accompany the Sydney Symphony Orchestra and Holst’s The Planets in a world-premiere performance.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with rape
item.description: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been charged with three counts of rape relating to alleged offending against a woman in her 30s at Burleigh Heads on March 30.

item.title: Construction ends on $915 million Parramatta Powerhouse museum set to open this year
item.description: Four years after work started, the highly anticipated Western Sydney museum is ready for the installation of five exhibitions, including a major show about space exploration.

item.title: Adelaide showed how good LIV Golf could be, but it was built on a flawed premise
item.description: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

item.title: From Foo Fighters to Jessie Ware and Nine Inch Noize — these are April's best albums
item.description: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

item.title: Future of Northern Territory ambulance service in doubt as paramedics reach breaking point
item.description: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

item.title: Victoria pledges to close legal 'loophole' to prevent attacks on paramedics
item.description: Ambulance Victoria and the paramedics union say some people who attack paramedics have escaped mandatory jail terms due to the law's definition of "on-duty".

item.title: A deadline for Trump to end the Iran war is looming. He may seek to get around it
item.description: A deadline for US President Donald Trump to end the Iran war is about to expire. Here is what to expect.

item.title: Jobs at stake at Nyrstar Port Pirie and Hobart as assistance package ends
item.description: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

item.title: Poliovirus linked to overseas transmission detected in Perth wastewater
item.description: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

item.title: No 'legal barrier' blocking ISIS-linked families returning to Australia, law expert says
item.description: A week since a group of 13 Australian women and children with ties to former Islamic State fighters left a Syrian refugee camp for Australia, they remain in the capital Damascus in a state of limbo.

item.title: Tense moment at FIFA Congress as Gianni Infantino tries to get Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands
item.description: FIFA boss Gianni Infantino is criticised as he attempts to encourage Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands.

item.title: More IP, more content, more jobs: What we learned at Screen Forever 40
item.description: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

item.title: SA Police confirm remains found at Inman Valley belong to Jeff Mundy
item.description: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

item.title: How NSW halfback Jesse Southwell, a future Brisbane hero, broke Queensland's hearts
item.description: In her final act as a Novocastrian before heading to Brisbane, Jesse Southwell kicked the first field goal of her life to deliver victory for New South Wales in Origin I.

item.title: Billions of NDIS funds lost to 'integrity leakage' last year, executive tells parliamentary hearing
item.description: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

item.title: Matt Glaezter, cancer survivor, world champion and two-time Olympic medallist, confirms retirement
item.description: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

item.title: Banksy confirmed behind new London statue of man blinded by flag
item.description: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

item.title: Wollongong tradesman jailed for robbing paedophile in Lightning Ridge heist
item.description: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

item.title: Outback WA shire demands trucks get off road after beloved teacher killed in crash
item.description: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

item.title: Family of allegedly murdered 5yo calls for calm as police reveal vigilante attack left suspect unconscious
item.description: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

item.title: Suns coach Damien Hardwick fires back at Saints' Ross Lyon's AFL draft comment
item.description: Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick provides a spirited response to St Kilda counterpart Ross Lyon's comment about the AFL national draft.

item.title: Supreme Court finds Perth man David Moussa not guilty of father's murder on insanity grounds
item.description: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

item.title: Port Pirie locals worried about jobs as Nyrstar smelter's future in doubt
item.description: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

item.title: Kingswood Primary School teacher accused of installing bathroom camera denied bail
item.description: Parents of children attending Kingswood Primary School filled the Moorabbin Magistrates Court on Friday, intending to see accused teacher Timothy Blamires.

item.title: Review finds fire ant eradication can succeed if flaws in 'mistrust and misinformation' are fixed
item.description: An independent review finds fire ant eradication is still achievable, but major issues, including poor communication, public mistrust and strategy confusion, must be fixed first.

item.title: Beaches closed near Wollongong as whale carcass removed at Bellambi
item.description: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

item.title: Kalgoorlie couple who underwent IVF treatment in shock after welcoming quadruplets
item.description: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

item.title: Retiring ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion
item.description: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

item.title: Melbourne Storm lose seven in a row for the first time in club history
item.description: The Melbourne Storm have sunk to a historic low, losing seven consecutive NRL matches for the first time as the Dolphins powered to victory at a wet Lang Park in Brisbane.

item.title: Rebel Wilson defamation trial told Charlotte MacInnes was 'fudging' her version of producer incident
item.description: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

item.title: Mother's grief after toddler dies in apparent murder-suicide in Lismore
item.description: A coronial inquest hears letters that arrived three days after an apparent murder-suicide in Lismore outlined a father's deadly plans.

item.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for killer Andrew Mungatopi
item.description: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

item.title: School traffic warden hit by delivery truck at Australind left with serious injuries
item.description: The man in his 80s was working a school zone crossing in Australind, near Bunbury in WA, when he was hit by the Woolworths truck.

item.title: NT government criticised for snubbing youth justice and incarceration Senate inquiry
item.description: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

item.title: Former Hunter high school teacher jailed for sexually assaulting teenage boy
item.description: A woman who groomed and sexually abused a teenage boy while she was working as a high school teacher near Newcastle has been sentenced to at least two years in prison.

item.title: Planning and Environment Court approves Sheraton high-rise development for Hervey Bay
item.description: Activists appealed the local council's approval of the 18-storey resort on the grounds that it would endanger local wildlife. The community fears the court's green light for the development will permanently change their seaside town.

item.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as unseasonably warm weather continues
item.description: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

item.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute as Freo downs Dogs
item.description: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

item.title: Chinese-owned Landbridge launches international legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin
item.description: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.

item.title: UK police charge man with stabbing attack on two Jewish Londoners
item.description: UK police charge a 45-year-old man over the stabbing of two Jewish men in north London, the latest in a string of attacks targeting Britain's Jewish community.

item.title: Gaza aid flotilla activists taken to Crete after Israeli interception
item.description: More than 100 activists, including six Australians, on ships bound for Gaza were intercepted by Israeli forces earlier this week.

item.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says
item.description: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.
